summary: This website has a solid foundation with meaningful improvement opportunities.

issues.title: No Social Proof on Website
issues.impact: 93% of consumers read reviews before purchasing. Sites with visible social proof convert at 2–4% vs. 0.5–1% for sites without. On 500 monthly visitors, that gap = 7–19 additional leads per month.
issues.description: Your site has no customer reviews, testimonials, or trust badges visible.
issues.recommendation: Add at least 3–5 customer testimonials to your homepage, plus a Google Reviews widget or star rating badge.

issues.title: No Content Security Policy header
issues.impact: Without a CSP, attackers can inject malicious scripts that steal user data, hijack sessions, or deface your site.
issues.govImpact: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 SC-18 (Mobile Code) + CMMC Level 1 AC.1.001 — Missing Content Security Policy allows XSS attacks; CMMC assessors flag absent security headers as IT hygiene failures during Level 2 pre-assessment interviews.
issues.description: Your site has no CSP header. Every form and session is exposed to cross-site scripting injection.
issues.recommendation: Add Content-Security-Policy response header on your server or CDN.

issues.title: HTTPS enforced but HSTS header missing
issues.impact: Without HSTS, SSL-strip attacks can silently redirect users to unencrypted connections.
issues.govImpact: CISA BOD 18-01 · NIST SP 800-52 Rev 2 — HTTPS not enforced via HSTS; man-in-the-middle attacks possible. CMMC Level 2 SC.3.177 requires encryption of CUI in transit.
issues.description: Your site uses HTTPS but does not send HSTS. Browsers can still be downgraded to HTTP by attackers.

issues.title: Accessibility critically failing — Section 508 risk
issues.impact: Inaccessible sites face ADA lawsuits and are excluded from federal contracting opportunities.
issues.govImpact: Section 508 §1194 + Rehabilitation Act 29 U.S.C. § 794d — ICT products must meet WCAG 2.1 AA; federal agencies cannot award ICT contracts without an Accessibility Conformance Report (VPAT). This finding causes automatic VPAT failure and bid disqualification.
issues.description: Multiple accessibility failures detected. Federal agencies and ADA Title III require accessible websites.
issues.recommendation: Add alt text to all images, label all form inputs, add lang attribute to <html>, ensure keyboard navigation works.

issues.title: WordPress site with unaudited third-party scripts
issues.impact: Compromised plugins or CDN scripts can inject malware affecting every site visitor.
issues.govImpact: EO 14028 (Improving the Nation's Cybersecurity) + NIST SP 800-161 Supply Chain Risk Management — CMMC Level 2 SR.3.169 requires organizations to assess supply chain risks of third-party components. WordPress with unaudited plugins is a documented CMMC assessment concern.
issues.description: WordPress detected with multiple scripts missing Subresource Integrity hashes. Supply chain injection risk.
issues.recommendation: Audit all plugins. Add integrity="" attributes to external scripts. Remove unused plugins immediately.

issues.title: No federal contractor credentials visible
issues.impact: Without verifiable credentials, contracting officers cannot award contracts regardless of past performance.
issues.govImpact: FAR 52.204-7 (SAM Registration) + FAR Subpart 9.1 (Responsibility Determinations) — 84% of government agencies research contractor websites before procurement discussions. Missing CAGE code, NAICS, and contract vehicles creates a responsibility red flag during pre-award due diligence.
issues.description: No SAM.gov registration, CAGE code, contract vehicles, or certifications found. Government buyers cannot verify eligibility.
issues.recommendation: Add a Capabilities or Contracts page listing SAM.gov UEI, CAGE code, NAICS codes, and any GSA schedules or certifications.

issues.title: Missing Meta Description
issues.impact: Pages with custom meta descriptions have 5.8% higher click-through rates on average, representing meaningful lost traffic for every search impression.
issues.description: Your page has no meta description. Google writes its own snippet — often poorly.
issues.recommendation: Write a 130–155 character meta description that explains what you do, who you serve, and includes a call to action.

issues.title: Phone Number Hidden From Header
issues.impact: Sites with header phone numbers see 30–50% more inbound calls than sites that bury the number. Phone leads close at 3x the rate of form leads.
issues.description: Your phone number doesn't appear in the header. Customers who want to call have to search for it.
issues.recommendation: Move your phone number to the top-right corner of every page header. Make it a clickable tel: link for mobile visitors.

issues.title: Form inputs missing accessible labels
issues.impact: Unlabeled forms are unusable for blind users and fail WCAG 2.1 Level A compliance.
issues.govImpact: Section 508 §1194.22(n) + WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 — Form fields without labels fail automated 508 testing. Required for VPAT Level AA conformance; this finding causes automatic VPAT failure and federal bid disqualification.
issues.description: One or more form inputs have no label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen readers cannot identify them.
issues.recommendation: Add <label for="inputId"> to every visible form input, or add aria-label attribute directly on the input.

issues.title: More Than Half of Images Missing Alt Text
issues.impact: Missing alt text means images contribute zero SEO value. Image search drives 22.6% of all web searches — every untagged image is invisible.
issues.description: 7 of your 13 images have no alt text. Search engines can't understand what they show.
issues.recommendation: Add descriptive alt text to every image: what it shows, and if relevant, your service/location keyword.

issues.title: No clickjacking protection header
issues.impact: Clickjacking attacks trick users into performing unintended actions on your site.
issues.govImpact: OWASP Top 10 A05:2021 (Security Misconfiguration) — Missing X-Frame-Options enables clickjacking. Federal IT procurement officers check for basic security header compliance as part of informal pre-award due diligence.
issues.description: Missing X-Frame-Options header. Your site can be embedded in iframes and used for clickjacking attacks.
issues.recommendation: Add X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN to response headers

issues.title: Missing ARIA landmark regions
issues.impact: Without landmarks, keyboard-only users must tab through every element to find content.
issues.govImpact: Section 508 + WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.6 — Missing ARIA landmarks prevent screen readers from navigating page structure. WCAG 2.1 AA is the federal baseline per the Access Board's 2025 ICT standards update; FTA January 2025 guidance specifically flags this for transit contractor websites.
issues.description: No <main> or <nav> landmark regions detected. Screen reader users cannot navigate page structure.
issues.recommendation: Wrap your main content in <main> and navigation in <nav>.

issues.title: No Structured Data (Schema Markup)
issues.impact: Rich results have 20–30% higher click-through rates than standard listings. Local businesses with schema get priority placement in Maps results.
issues.description: Your site has no schema markup. This is code that helps Google display rich results (star ratings, hours, FAQs) in search.
issues.recommendation: Add LocalBusiness schema with your name, address, phone, hours, and service area. Many SEO plugins handle this automatically.

topFix.body: Your phone number exists on your site but isn't in the header where visitors look first. Move it to the top-right of your navigation on every page. On mobile, make it a tap-to-call link: <a href='tel:+1XXXXXXXXXX'>.
topFix.headline: Move your phone number to the top of every page
topFix.nuStackHook: NuStack can make this change across your entire site in under an hour.
topFix.impactStatement: Phone numbers in the header increase inbound call volume by an average of 30% for service businesses because mobile visitors won't hunt for contact information.
